reflection.summary: User requested a 3-level nested delegation (CEO → Division X → Worker Y) to create a dummy file, but the tooling only supports 2 levels and had a parameter conflict, so the task was completed via direct delegation instead.
errors: TypeError: tools.delegate_tool.delegate_task() got multiple values for keyword argument 'parent_agent' when calling abelion_delegate for nested delegation attempt
abelion_delegate does not support nesting — it is a 2-level wrapper (CEO → Division → Worker) and Division X cannot re-invoke abelion_delegate to spawn Worker Y
lessons: abelion_delegate is a fixed-depth (2-level) wrapper over delegate_task; do not attempt to chain/nest multiple abelion_delegate calls
abelion_delegate internally sets parent_agent; passing it explicitly again causes a duplicate-keyword argument error — check tool signatures before re-wrapping
When user requests multi-level delegation that exceeds tool depth, flatten and execute directly rather than attempting unsupported nesting
Delegate leaf tasks (file write) directly to the worker level in a single delegation call rather than splitting across two hops if the intermediate division adds no value
recommendations: Add a skill/memory note on abelion_delegate's depth limit (max_spawn_depth=1) and the parent_agent parameter conflict to avoid repeated probing
Clarify in the agent prompt that abelion_delegate is a convenience wrapper with fixed semantics — not a general-purpose recursive dispatch primitive
When a user's delegation topology exceeds tool limits, proactively suggest a flattened plan before attempting and failing at nested calls
